page: Microsoft Azure DC48ads_v5 is a dedicated virtual machine instance belonging to the DCADSv5 server family. Powered by an AMD processor with x86_64 architecture, it features 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. The instance operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration includes 1843 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It does not feature any integrated GPUs, making it a non-accelerated, CPU-centric resource. The instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local storage capacity, the DC48ads_v5 offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources without the premium cost of GPU accelerators. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, database hosting, and enterprise workloads that benefit from local disk storage and dedicated CPU allocation.
description: Microsoft Azure DC48ads_v5 is a dedicated virtual machine from the DCADSv5 family, built on x86_64 AMD processors and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. This instance is Premium Storage capable and does not include integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its dedicated CPU allocation and substantial local disk capacity, the DC48ads_v5 is designed for resource-dense workloads. It is well-suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, database management, and workloads requiring consistent compute performance and local storage without the need for hardware acceleration.
